title: Ophrys liveranii
taxonomy: Ophrys liveranii is a species of orchid in the genus Ophrys. It is a type of bee orchid, known for its unique appearance and ability to mimic female bees to attract pollinators. The classification of Ophrys liveranii is as follows: it belongs to the family Orchidaceae, the order Asparagales, the class Liliopsida, the division Magnoliophyta, and the kingdom Plantae. This species is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Ophrys liveranii is a perennial plant that produces one or more flowers in the spring and summer months.

intro: Ophrys liveranii, also known as Liver Orchid, is a rare and endemic orchid species found in the Mediterranean region. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential applications in treating various gastrointestinal issues and supporting liver health. The bioactive constituents of Ophrys liveranii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions and infusions, which are often used in traditional medicine to support liver function and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders.
